req.title: Run payroll — variant 3
req.statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path payroll completion with gross/deduction/net computation, pay-stub creation, audit entry, and webhook emission; (2) conflict guard when a run is already in progress (409); (3) atomic rollback on engine failure (500, no partial commit); (4) boundary of exactly one active employee (minimum non-empty cohort). State transitions idle→running→completed and idle→running→failed are also exercised. Tests are scoped to the /api/payroll-runs REST endpoint with an hr-admin bearer token supplied via fixture.

tests.name: test_payroll_run_completes_for_active_employees
preconditions: Caller holds role hr-admin (bearer token from <hr_admin_token> fixture)
At least two active employees exist with salary data
No payroll run is currently in progress
Webhook receiver endpoint is reachable (<webhook_sink> fixture)
actions: Poll GET /api/payroll-runs/{id} until state=completed or timeout 30 s
expected: POST returns 201 with body containing id and state=queued|running
Polled state reaches completed
pay-stubs collection contains one record per active employee
Each pay-stub has gross_pay > 0, deductions >= 0, net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
Audit log contains exactly one entry referencing the run id
Webhook sink received exactly one event with type=payroll.completed and run_id matching
trace: R-WEB-074: webhook event emitted for external consumers
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time

def test_payroll_run_completes_for_active_employees(
    hr_admin_client,   # <fixture>: requests.Session with hr-admin bearer token
    seed_two_employees,  # <fixture>: ensures 2 active employees exist, yields employee ids
    webhook_sink,      # <fixture>: captures POST events, exposes .events list
    api_base,          # <fixture>: base URL string
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs",
        json={"period": "2024-05", "type": "monthly"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["id"]

    state = <poll_until_terminal>(hr_admin_client, api_base, run_id, timeout=30)
    assert state == "completed"

    stubs = hr_admin_client.get(f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/pay-stubs").json()
    assert len(stubs) == len(seed_two_employees)
    for stub in stubs:
        assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
        assert stub["deductions"] >= 0
        assert stub["net_pay"] == pytest.approx(stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"])

    audit = hr_admin_client.get(f"{api_base}/api/audit-log?ref={run_id}").json()
    assert len(audit["entries"]) >= 1

    matched = [e for e in webhook_sink.events if e.get("run_id") == run_id]
    assert len(matched) == 1
    assert matched[0]["type"] == "payroll.completed"

tests.name: test_payroll_run_returns_409_when_run_already_in_progress
preconditions: A payroll run for period 2024-05 is currently in state=running (seeded via <fixture>)
No completed run for the same period exists
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with body {"period": "2024-05", "type": "monthly"} while a run is already in progress
expected: Response status is 409 Conflict
Response body contains error code PAYROLL_RUN_IN_PROGRESS
No new payroll run record is created
No webhook event is emitted
trace: R-WEB-074: 409 if another run is in progress
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_returns_409_when_run_already_in_progress(
    hr_admin_client,
    running_payroll_run,  # <fixture>: seeds a payroll run in state=running for 2024-05, yields run_id
    webhook_sink,
    api_base,
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs",
        json={"period": "2024-05", "type": "monthly"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") == "PAYROLL_RUN_IN_PROGRESS"

    runs = hr_admin_client.get(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs?period=2024-05"
    ).json()["items"]
    run_ids = [r["id"] for r in runs]
    assert run_ids == [running_payroll_run], "duplicate run must not be created"

    duplicate_events = [
        e for e in webhook_sink.events
        if e.get("run_id") != running_payroll_run
    ]
    assert duplicate_events == []

tests.name: test_payroll_run_atomic_rollback_on_engine_failure
preconditions: Payroll engine fault-injection is enabled via <fault_injection_header> or feature flag
No run is currently in progress
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with fault-injection header to force engine failure mid-run
Poll GET /api/payroll-runs/{id} until state=failed or timeout 30 s
expected: POST returns 202 or 201 (run accepted before failure occurs)
Polled state eventually equals failed
pay-stubs collection is empty (no partial commit)
Audit log records the failure with state=failed
No payroll.completed webhook event emitted; optionally a payroll.failed event may exist
trace: R-WEB-074: 500 rolled back atomically on failure
R-WEB-074: failed run shall not partially commit
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_atomic_rollback_on_engine_failure(
    hr_admin_client,
    seed_two_employees,
    webhook_sink,
    api_base,
    fault_injection_headers,  # <fixture>: dict with header that forces engine fault
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs",
        json={"period": "2024-06", "type": "monthly"},
        headers=fault_injection_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (201, 202)
    run_id = resp.json()["id"]

    state = <poll_until_terminal>(hr_admin_client, api_base, run_id, timeout=30)
    assert state == "failed"

    stubs = hr_admin_client.get(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/pay-stubs"
    ).json()
    assert stubs["items"] == [], "partial pay-stubs must be rolled back"

    audit = hr_admin_client.get(f"{api_base}/api/audit-log?ref={run_id}").json()
    failed_entries = [e for e in audit["entries"] if e.get("state") == "failed"]
    assert len(failed_entries) >= 1

    completed_events = [
        e for e in webhook_sink.events
        if e.get("type") == "payroll.completed" and e.get("run_id") == run_id
    ]
    assert completed_events == []

tests.name: test_payroll_run_with_exactly_one_active_employee
preconditions: Exactly one active employee exists with valid salary record
All other employees are inactive or absent
No payroll run is in progress
expected: POST returns 201
State reaches completed
Exactly one pay-stub record exists
Pay-stub net_pay equals gross_pay minus deductions and is greater than zero
Webhook emits exactly one payroll.completed event
trace: R-WEB-074: boundary — minimum valid employee cohort (N=1)
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_with_exactly_one_active_employee(
    hr_admin_client,
    seed_one_employee,   # <fixture>: ensures exactly 1 active employee, deactivates others, yields emp_id
    webhook_sink,
    api_base,
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs",
        json={"period": "2024-07", "type": "monthly"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["id"]

    state = <poll_until_terminal>(hr_admin_client, api_base, run_id, timeout=30)
    assert state == "completed"

    stubs = hr_admin_client.get(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/pay-stubs"
    ).json()["items"]
    assert len(stubs) == 1, "exactly one stub for one active employee"

    stub = stubs[0]
    assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
    assert stub["net_pay"] == pytest.approx(stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"])
    assert stub["employee_id"] == seed_one_employee

    events = [e for e in webhook_sink.events if e.get("run_id") == run_id]
    assert len(events) == 1
    assert events[0]["type"] == "payroll.completed"

tests.name: test_payroll_run_unauthorized_role_rejected
preconditions: No payroll run is in progress
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with body {"period": "2024-05", "type": "monthly"} using employee-role token
expected: Response status is 403 Forbidden
Response body contains error indicating insufficient permissions
No payroll run record is created
No webhook event is emitted
trace: R-WEB-074: precondition role:hr-admin — non-admin must be denied
R-WEB-074: decision table row: actor=employee → reject
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_unauthorized_role_rejected(
    employee_client,   # <fixture>: requests.Session with employee (non-admin) bearer token
    webhook_sink,
    api_base,
):
    initial_runs = employee_client.get(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs?period=2024-05"
    )
    # 403 may also be returned for list; track count if accessible
    before_count = (
        len(initial_runs.json().get("items", []))
        if initial_runs.status_code == 200 else None
    )

    resp = employee_client.post(
        f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs",
        json={"period": "2024-05", "type": "monthly"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

    if before_count is not None:
        after = employee_client.get(
            f"{api_base}/api/payroll-runs?period=2024-05"
        ).json().get("items", [])
        assert len(after) == before_count, "no new run must be created"

    rogue_events = [
        e for e in webhook_sink.events
        if e.get("type") == "payroll.completed"
    ]
    assert rogue_events == []
